detaileddescription.para: GPIO PS register offset for masked data write of bank 3

briefdescription.para: GPIO PS register offset for direction mode of bank 3.
GPIO PS register offset for output enable of bank 3.
GPIO PS register offset for data of bank 3.
Define GIC Device ID based on platform.

detaileddescription.para: GPIO base address SDT GPIO base address mapping

memberdef.name: useCase

briefdescription.para: List of test use cases with video configuration parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This function drives the reset pin of the MultiScaler core by toggling the appropriate GPIO register. It performs a reset sequence to initialize the core to a known state.

simplesect.para: On systems with XPAR_GPIO_0_BASEADDR, uses AXI GPIO. Otherwise, uses PS GPIO (XGPIOPS) registers.

briefdescription.para: Callback function for MultiScaler interrupt handling.

detaileddescription.para: This function is called from the interrupt handler of the MultiScaler core when processing completes. It clears the interrupt flag, disables interrupts, and stops the MultiScaler core.

simplesect.para: This callback is registered during initialization and is invoked when the MultiScaler completes a frame processing operation.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the stride for a given color format and width.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the memory stride (line pitch) in bytes based on the color format, AXI memory-mapped data width, and image width. It accounts for different bits-per-pixel ratios and ensures proper alignment.

parameterdescription.para: AXI memory-mapped data width in bits

simplesect.para: The calculated stride is aligned to the AXI data width boundary.

detaileddescription.para:
This function demonstrates the MultiScaler IP functionality by executing predefined test cases. It initializes the system, configures the MultiScaler, processes test frames, and verifies the output. The function performs the following steps:
Disable caches and exceptions
Initialize the MultiScaler IP
Setup interrupt system
Execute test cases
Verify output data integrity

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all test cases pass successfully
XST_FAILURE if initialization or any test case fails

simplesect.para: This example assumes the MultiScaler IP is present in the hardware design and memory buffers are accessible at defined addresses.
